title: Latent two-part polyurethane adhesives curable under ambient conditions
abstract: Disclosed are latent two-part polyurethane adhesives that rapidly develop adhesive strength when cured under ambient conditions containing a polyol component which includes; one or more polyols having a hydroxyl equivalent weight of 400 to 2000 and a nominal hydroxyl functionality of 2 to 4; one or more aliphatic diol chain extenders; and one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts; b) a polyisocyanate component containing one or more polyisocyanate compounds; and a blocked amine including a tertiary amine or a secondary ketimine blocked with a boron containing compound. Further disclosed are methods of bonding structures together using the latent two-part polyurethane adhesives.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A composition comprising two parts including a Part 1 and a Part 2: wherein the Part 1 comprises; a) one or more polyisocyanates including two or more isocyanate groups: and the Part 2 comprises; b1 ) one or more isocyanate reactive compounds containing isocyanate reactive groups: and b2) one or more blocked compounds including a blocked amine group including an amine group blocked by a boron containing compound having a boron atom, wherein the blocked amine group is: i) a tertiary amine having a nitrogen atom in association with the boron atom, or it) a secondary ketimine having a nitrogen atom in association with a boron atom; wherein the blocked amine group dissociates from the boron atom upon contact with the one or more poiyisocyanates to form the boron containing compound and an unblocked compound including one or more unblocked amine groups that catalyze a reaction of the isocyanates groups with the isocyanate reactive groups, wherein the unblocked amine group is the tertiary amine or the secondary ketimine. 2. The composition of claim 1 , wherein the boron containing compound has a formula: wherein B represents Boron; H represents Hydrogen; X is independently in each occurrence a halogen, an alkoxy, a carboxylate or an acetate group; R independently in each occurrence a lower alkyl group; a is independently in each occurrence an integer of from 1 to 3; b is independently in each occurrence an integer of from 0 to 2; and c is independently in each occurrence an integer of from 0 to 1 ; wherein either or both of b or c are 0; preferably R is separately in each occurrence a C i-s lower alkyi group, more preferably R is separately in each occurrence a C lower alkyl group, even more preferably R is separately in each occurrence methyl or ethyl; preferably, wherein the composition cures upon contacting Part 1 and Part 2 at room temperature and the open time is from about 1 to about 30 minutes. 3. The composition of claim 1 or 2, wherein the boron containing compound is borane <BH 3 ); preferably the one or more blocked compounds are present in Part 2, more preferably in an amount of about 0.05 to about 5 percent by weigh, even more preferably in an amount of about 0.10 to about 2 percent by weight, and most preferably in an amount of about 0.20 to about 1 percent by weight. 4. The composition of any of claims 1 through 3, wherein the blocked compound Includes more than one blocked amine groups; preferably wherein all of the available tertiary amines and all of the available secondary ketimine are blocked (for example, amines that are sterically hindered may not be available for blocking, such as a nitrogen atom that forms two rings). 5. The composition of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the blocked compound includes an aromatic or cycloaliphatic structure, having one or more rings, wherein the nitrogen atom of the blocked amine group is pendant from the aromatic or cycloaliphatic structure, or the nitrogen atom is incorporated into the one or more rings. 6. The composition of claim 5, wherein the blocked compound has the aromatic structure including an aromatic ring and includes the tertiary amine, wherein the tertiary amine is disposed on an alkyl group bound to the aromatic ring; preferably, wherein the blocked compound includes a plurality of tertiary amines, preferably the tertiary amines are each disposed on alky groups which are each bound to the same aromatic ring. 7. The composition of claim 5, wherein the blocked compound includes the secondary ketimine; preferably wherein the blocked compound contains a cyclic amidine structure including the secondary ketamine. 8. The composition of claim 5, wherein the unblocked compound is 1 ,8-diazabicycloundec- 7-ene (DBU), 1 ,5-Diazabicyclo[4.3.0]non-5-ene (DBN) or tris1 ,3,5-(2(N,N-dimethyl amino) ethyl) benzene. 9. The composition of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the composition includes one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts, preferably including tin, zinc or bismuth; more preferably including zinc alkanoates, bismuth alkanoates, dialkyltin alkanoates, dialkyl tin mercaptides, dialkyl tin bis(alkylmercaptoacetates), dialkyltin thioglycolates or mixtures thereof; even more preferably including dialkyl tin mercaptides, dialkyl tin bis(alky!mercaptoacetates), dialkyltin thioglycolates or mixtures thereof; preferably wherein the one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts are disposed in Part 2; preferably wherein the one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts are present in an amount of about 0.005 to about 1.0 percent by weight based on the weight of the polyol component and/or the one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts are present in an amount of about 0.020 to about 0.050 percent by weight based on the weight of Part 2. 10. The composition of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the composition includes one or more latent room temperature organometallic catalysts including zinc alkanoates, bismuth 

 alkanoates, dialkyltin alkanoates, dialkyl tin mercaptides, dialky! tin bis(alkylmercaptoacetates), dialkyltin thioglycolates or mixtures thereof. 11. The composition of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein i) the isocyanate reactive compounds comprise one or more low molecular weight isocyanate reactive compounds and one or more poiyalkylene oxides containing two or more isocyanate reactive groups; or the isocyanate reactive groups are hydroxyl groups and/or amino groups; preferably wherein the one or more one or more poiyalkylene oxides containing two or more isocyanate reactive groups are present in an amount of about 35 to about 80 percent by weight of Part 2, the one or more low molecular weight isocyanate reactive compounds are present in an amount of about 1 to about 10 percent by weight of Part 2, and, one or more of the polyisocyanates are present in an amount of about 20 to about 65 percent by weight of Part 1. 12. A composition of any of claims 1 to 11 , wherein Part 1 , Part 2, or both contains one or more particulate fillers; preferably wherein the particulate filler is present in an amount of about 10 to about 50 percent of the weight in Part 1 or the particulate filler is present in an amount of about 25 to about 60 percent of the weight of Part 2. 13. A method comprising the steps of: a) contacting the Part 1 and the Part 2 of the composition of any of claims 1 through 12, and mixing to form a mixture; b) applying the mixture to a first substrate; and c) contacting a second substrate with the first substrate with the mixture of part a disposed between the first and second substrate; preferably wherein the mixture is a homogeneous mixture. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the homogeneous mixture of step a cures without exposure to heat; preferably the mixture cures under ambient conditions. 15. The method of claim 13 or 14, wherein the mixture of step a is exposed to a temperature of about 15 Â°C to about 50 "C during cure; preferably about 20 Â°C to about 35 Â°C during cure. 16. The method of any of claims 13 to 15, wherein the open time of the mixture of step a is from 1 to 30 minutes. 17. A method according to any one of Claims 30 to 35 wherein the first substrate and second substrate are bonded together upon cure of the mixture of step a. preferably, wherein the first substrate and second substrate bonded together by the mixture of step a can be handled; preferably wherein the first substrate and the second substrate are dissimilar substrates, more preferably wherein one or both substrates comprise metal, fiber reinforced polymers, coated metal, polymers or coated polymers. 18. An article comprising: a first substrate and a second substrate bonded together by a composition according to any one of Claims 1 to 12 wherein contacted and cured Part 1 and Part 2 are disposed between the first substrate and the second substrate. 19. A composition comprising one or more blocked compounds including a blocked amine group blocked by a boron containing compound having a boron atom, wherein the blocked amine group is i) a tertiary amine having a nitrogen atom in association with the boron atom, or ii) a secondary ketimine having a nitrogen atom in association with the boron atom; wherein each tertiary amine and each secondary ketimine is blocked with the boron containing compound; wherein the blocked compound includes an aromatic or cycloaliphatic structure, having one or more rings, wherein the nitrogen atom is pendant from the aromatic or cycloaliphatic structure, or the nitrogen atom is incorporated into the one or more rings; prefe rmulas: wherein B represents Boron; H represents Hydrogen; X is independently in each occurrence a halogen, alkoxy, carboxylate or acetate group; R is independently in each occurrence a lower alky! group; R 2 is independently in each occurrence one or more cyclic aliphatic or aromatic rings, which may contain one or more hydrocarbyl groups bonded to the one or more rings and one or more heteroatoms in the cyclic rings or the hydrocarblyl groups, arranged such that the nitrogen atom is tertiary, has three bonds to it; R 3 is independently in each occurrence a hydrocarbyl group which may contain one or more heteroatoms; R 4 is independently in each occurrence a lower alkyl group; a is independently in each occurrence an integer of from 1 to 3; b is independently in each occurrence a integer of from 0 to 2; c is independently in each occurrence a integer of from 0 to 1 ; wherein either or both of b or c are 0; d is independently in each occurence an integer of 0 to 2; and e is independently in each occurrence an integer of from 1 to 3.
